title: A Worked Training Step Example
concept: Concrete walkthrough: pick a clean image, pick a random timestep, add the corresponding noise, ask the network to predict that noise, compute loss. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: The U-Net Backbone for Diffusion.'

exercises.prompt: Before training, what does a diffusion model need to learn to predict?

options.text: The noise that was added to a picture
The name of the picture's artist
The file size of the picture

exercises.explain: That's the whole trick! The model learns to spot the noise hiding in a noisy picture so it can later remove it.

cards.title: One Step, Start to Finish
cards.body: Ever wonder what actually happens inside ONE training moment for a diffusion model? Let's walk across the bridge together — from a clean photo to a single number called 'loss'. No magic, just simple steps!

cards.title: The Training Step, Step by Step
cards.body:
1) Grab a clean image.
2) Pick a random timestep (a number saying 'how noisy').
3) Add exactly that much noise to the image.
4) Show the noisy image to the network and ask: 'What noise do you see?'
5) Compare the network's guess to the real noise added.
6) That difference is the loss — a score of how wrong the guess was.

cards.title: Like a Guess-the-Fog Game
cards.body: Imagine someone sprays a random amount of fog on a photo and hides how much they sprayed. You guess exactly what the fog looks like. Then they reveal the real fog and check how close your guess was. Do this a million times, and you get amazing at spotting fog patterns!

cards.title: A Bird Photo Example
cards.body: Start with a clear photo of a bird. Randomly pick timestep 400 (medium noise). Add that exact noise to the bird photo. The network looks at the noisy bird and predicts what the noise pattern was. We compare its guess to the real noise — the smaller the difference, the smaller the loss, the better the network did.

cards.title: Myth: The Model Predicts the Clean Image Directly
cards.body: Actually no! In this training step, the model isn't asked to draw the clean bird photo. It's only asked to predict the NOISE that was added. Predicting noise turns out to be an easier, more stable target for the network to learn.

cards.title: Two Different Jobs
cards.body: Comparing what the model predicts during this training step versus what it does NOT predict.

left.text: The noise pattern hidden in the noisy image

exercises.prompt: In this one training step, what does the network try to predict?

options.text: The noise added to the image
The color palette of the image

exercises.explain: Correct! The network's one job during training is to guess the exact noise that was mixed into the clean image.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The timestep chosen for each training step is picked randomly.
exercises.explain: True! Picking a random timestep each time helps the network practice handling every noise level, from barely foggy to totally noisy.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the training step.
exercises.sentence: After adding noise, the network compares its guess to the real noise to compute the ___.
exercises.explain: The loss is the score that tells the network how far off its noise guess was, so it can improve next time.

exercises.prompt: Which of these best describes the correct order of a single training step?

options.text: Clean image → pick timestep → add noise → predict noise → compute loss
Add noise → clean image → compute loss → predict noise
Predict noise → clean image → pick timestep → add noise

exercises.explain: Right! You always start clean, decide how noisy to make it, apply that noise, ask for a noise guess, then score the guess.

exercises.prompt: A student says: 'During training, the network looks at a clean image and a noisy image side-by-side, then predicts the clean image directly.' What's wrong with this statement?

options.text: The network only sees the noisy image and predicts the noise that was added, not the clean image
Nothing is wrong, that's exactly how it works
The network never sees any image at all
The network predicts the timestep number instead of noise

exercises.explain: Exactly! The network only sees the noisy image and must predict the noise hidden inside it — not the clean picture and not the timestep. Next up: The U-Net Backbone for Diffusion.
